name: cache

entries.title: Production concurrent cache: public API
entries.summary: How to USE a high-performance concurrent cache in Go. The generic Cache[K comparable, V any] exposes Get, Set, SetWithTTL, Delete, Range, Len, Close and Stats; LoadingCache[K,V].Get(ctx, key) loads on

entries.title: Cache builder and configuration
entries.summary: Configure and build a concurrent cache: total capacity (max cost), a per-entry cost function, a removal/eviction listener, default TTL, and turning it into a LoadingCache with a loader function. Keywo

entries.title: Count-min sketch frequency estimator
entries.summary: A probabilistic frequency counter (count-min sketch) used by TinyLFU to approximate per-key access frequency in fixed memory, with periodic aging/halving. Keywords: count-min count min sketch frequenc

entries.title: Reader-biased RWMutex (read-heavy lock)
entries.summary: A read-write mutex optimized for read-heavy workloads (reader-biased): far cheaper reads than sync.RWMutex when many goroutines read concurrently and writes are rare - the correct lock for a high-read

entries.title: Single-flight: cache stampede protection
entries.summary: Collapse concurrent duplicate loads of the SAME key into one in-flight call, so a burst of cache misses for one key triggers exactly ONE origin fetch and all callers share the result (thundering-herd

entries.title: Segmented LRU (SLRU) eviction
entries.summary: Segmented LRU: a probation segment and a protected segment, so frequently-reused entries survive longer than one-hit-wonders; the main cache structure under TinyLFU. Keywords: slru segmented lru evict

entries.title: TTL expiry via a hashed timer wheel
entries.summary: Expire many cache entries efficiently with a hierarchical hashed timer wheel instead of one timer per entry or scanning the whole map - the correct, scalable way to do TTL/expiration in a large cache.

entries.title: W-TinyLFU admission/eviction policy
entries.summary: Window-TinyLFU: a small admission window plus a main segmented-LRU cache, with a frequency sketch deciding whether to admit a new entry over the eviction victim - high hit ratio at bounded memory. Key
